title: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series - Power Output Faults or Generator Error Codes
description: Guide for troubleshooting CONMED System 2450 / 5000 ESU power faults and generator error codes, prioritizing external checks and safety.

steps.title: Verify Power Source
steps.instructions: Ensure the ESU is connected to a properly rated electrical outlet. Check for tripped circuit breakers or blown fuses. Use a known-good outlet or power meter to confirm adequate voltage. Why: Inconsistent power can trigger generator fault codes.

steps.title: Check Power Cord and Connections
steps.instructions: Inspect the main power cord for cuts, frays, or bent pins. Confirm secure connections at both the wall outlet and ESU. Why: Loose or damaged cords can cause intermittent faults.

steps.title: Examine Footswitch and Handpiece Connections
steps.instructions: Disconnect and reconnect the footswitch and handpiece cables. Swap with known-good accessories if available. Why: Faulty accessories can trigger output errors or prevent energy delivery.

steps.title: Inspect Electrodes and Patient Return Electrode (Pad)
steps.instructions: Ensure the correct electrode type is installed and compatible. Check the patient return electrode for proper placement and intact connection. Replace pads if adhesive or conductive gel is compromised. Why: Poor contact increases impedance, leading to generator error codes.

steps.title: Review Settings and Mode Selection
steps.instructions: Confirm the ESU is in the correct mode (cut, coag, blend, etc.) for the procedure. Reset any custom presets or safety lockouts that may prevent output. Why: Incorrect mode selection can simulate a power fault.

steps.title: Check Display for Specific Error Codes
steps.instructions: Note the exact error code(s) displayed. Reference the manufacturer’s external troubleshooting chart (do not attempt internal repair). Why: Error codes help determine if escalation is needed.

steps.title: Perform Basic Functional Test (if safe)
steps.instructions: With the ESU powered on and no patient connected, verify that the unit passes self-test routines or outputs low-level energy to a dummy load (per manufacturer safe procedure). Why: Confirms if the issue is isolated to the generator or external connections.

CCR.Complaint: OR staff reported that the ESU displayed “Output Fault” and would not deliver energy during surgery.
CCR.Cause: Inspected power source, cords, footswitch, handpiece, electrodes, and settings; all appeared normal. Generator error code persisted.
CCR.Resolution: Unit removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for authorized repair.

helpfulDetails: Verified outlet voltage and power cord integrity.
Error codes noted exactly as displayed.
Self-test and dummy load checks performed (if safe).

title: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series - Footswitch Activation Failure
description: Guide for troubleshooting footswitch activation failure on a CONMED System 2450 / 5000 ESU, focusing on external checks before internal repair.

steps.title: Ensure ESU is Powered On and Idle
steps.instructions:
Verify the generator is turned on and has completed its self-check.
Confirm the device is not in a lockout mode or alarm state, which can prevent footswitch activation.
Why: Footswitchs will not activate if the ESU is not ready to deliver energy.

steps.title: Inspect Footswitch Connection
steps.instructions:
Disconnect and reconnect the footswitch cable.
Examine the connector for bent pins, debris, or damage.
If using a multi-function footswitch, ensure the correct port is being used.
Why: Poor connection is a common cause of activation failure.

steps.title: Test with Alternate Footswitch (If Available)
steps.instructions:
Swap with a known-good footswitch to isolate the issue to the footswitch or the generator.
Why: Confirms whether the problem is the accessory or the ESU input.

steps.title: Check Footswitch Functionality
steps.instructions:
Depress the footswitch while visually observing the generator’s indicators.
Listen for the click or tactile feedback from the footswitch.
Why: Confirms that the mechanical action is being properly transmitted.

steps.title: Inspect Cables for Damage or Kinks
steps.instructions:
Look for fraying, cuts, or sharp bends in the footswitch cable.
Replace the cable if any damage is found.
Why: Broken wiring inside the cable can prevent activation.

steps.title: Verify Generator Input Settings
steps.instructions:
Ensure the ESU is configured for footswitch operation in the system menu.
Some models allow selection between handpiece activation, footswitch, or dual.
Why: Incorrect settings can disable footswitch input.

CCR.Complaint: Footswitch does not activate generator energy output when depressed.
CCR.Cause: Footswitch cable found connected properly but activation failed; mechanical action not registering on generator.
CCR.Resolution: Tested with alternate footswitch; issue persisted. Device removed from service and labeled Out of Service. Sent for vendor repair.

helpfulDetails: Confirmation of generator settings for footswitch operation.

title: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series - Handpiece Port Damage or Loose Connection
description: Guide for troubleshooting a CONMED ESU when the handpiece port is damaged or loose, focusing on external checks before repair.

steps.title: Power Down the ESU
steps.instructions: Ensure the ESU is turned off and unplugged before inspecting the handpiece port. Why: Prevents accidental energy delivery and protects both the technician and device.

steps.title: Inspect the Handpiece Connector
steps.instructions: Look for bent pins, corrosion, broken or missing parts, and foreign debris. Check the alignment and integrity of the connector. Why: Physical damage or debris can prevent proper contact and energy transfer.

steps.title: Check the Handpiece Itself
steps.instructions: Confirm the handpiece plug is fully seated and not damaged. Swap with a known good handpiece if available. Why: Determines if the problem lies with the handpiece or the port.

steps.title: Examine for Loose Port Mounting
steps.instructions: Gently wiggle the port to check for looseness in the housing. Why: A loose port can cause intermittent energy delivery or failure.

steps.title: Test Device Operation (Without Patient)
steps.instructions: Reconnect a verified good handpiece and power on the unit. Attempt a brief energy activation using the footswitch. Why: Confirms whether the port is now making consistent contact.

steps.title: Check Alarms or Error Codes
steps.instructions: Observe if the ESU displays a “handpiece error” or similar code. Why: Confirms that the system recognizes a connection fault.

CCR.Complaint: Handpiece will not deliver energy; connector feels loose.
CCR.Cause: Observed handpiece port pins slightly bent and connector housing loose. Handpiece swap did not resolve issue.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent to biomedical service for port repair.

helpfulDetails: Physical condition: Pins bent, housing loose
Final device status: Out of Service

title: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series - Internal Cooling Fan Failure Causing Thermal Shutdown
description: Troubleshoot CONMED ESU shutting down due to internal thermal protection, likely caused by fan failure or airflow obstruction.

steps.title: Ensure the device is powered off and unplugged
steps.instructions:
Safety first: thermal shutdown may indicate internal overheating.
Prevents accidental burns or electrical hazards.

steps.title: Inspect the external vents and fan intake areas
steps.instructions:
Check for dust, lint, or surgical drape debris obstructing airflow.
Blocked vents can mimic fan failure by causing overheating.

steps.title: Verify ambient operating conditions
steps.instructions:
Ensure the ESU is not in a confined space near heat sources.
High ambient temperature can trigger thermal shutdown.

steps.title: Check for error codes on the display
steps.instructions:
CONMED may show a specific fan or thermal fault code.
Document codes for CCR entry and potential service escalation.

steps.title: Inspect external fan assembly (if accessible)
steps.instructions:
Some models allow removal of external fan covers.
Check for loose connectors, broken blades, or abnormal resistance when spun by hand.

steps.title: Power on device in a controlled environment
steps.instructions:
With vents clear, monitor for fan start-up and normal sound.
If fan fails to spin or overheating recurs within minutes, internal fan likely failed.

CCR.Complaint: ESU shuts down unexpectedly with thermal error during operation.
CCR.Cause: Observed that internal cooling fan did not activate; vents clear, power stable.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for manufacturer repair.

helpfulDetails: Ambient temperature at time of shutdown
Condition of vents and fan housing
Any unusual sounds or smells observed

title: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series - Front Panel Membrane Keypad Failure
description: Guide for troubleshooting unresponsive or intermittent front panel membrane keypad on a CONMED System 2450 / 5000 ESU.

steps.title: Verify Device Power
steps.instructions: Confirm the ESU is plugged into a verified, live outlet. Ensure the device powers on normally; check the display and status LEDs. Why: A low or unstable voltage can cause keypad misbehavior.

steps.title: Check for Locked or Disabled Controls
steps.instructions: Some ESUs have a “lock” function or require activation mode for keypad use. Refer to on-screen indicators for keypad lock or settings. Why: Users may inadvertently lock the keypad, mimicking failure.

steps.title: Inspect Keypad Surface and Cleanliness
steps.instructions: Visually check for debris, liquids, or wear that may prevent key actuation. Use a dry microfiber cloth to clean the membrane surface. Avoid liquids unless manufacturer-approved. Why: Dirt or moisture can cause missed inputs.

steps.title: Check for Physical Damage
steps.instructions: Look for peeling, cracks, or separation between the keypad membrane and front panel. Gently press all keys to detect non-responsive areas. Why: Physical damage often leads to localized failures.

steps.title: Verify Cable Connections (if externally connected)
steps.instructions: Some models may have an internal ribbon or connector accessible from service panels. Ensure the connection is seated firmly without visible corrosion or bent pins. Why: Loose connectors can cause intermittent keypad response.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Turn the unit off, wait 30–60 seconds, and turn it back on. Observe if the keypad responds after reboot. Why: Resets may clear minor electronic or firmware glitches.

steps.title: Review Error or Diagnostic Logs
steps.instructions: Check if the ESU displays keypad-related error codes. Note any warnings for future repair escalation. Why: Confirms whether the failure is detected internally.

CCR.Complaint: Front panel keys unresponsive; cannot select settings.
CCR.Cause: Membrane keypad unresponsive to physical input; no debris or lock detected.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service and sent for repair; work order documented for follow-up.

helpfulDetails: Device power confirmed and outlet tested
Membrane surface visually inspected and cleaned
Ribbon cable or connector checked (if accessible)
Final device status: Out of Service

title: CONMED Hall Power Tools Surgical Power Tool System - Handpiece Not Recognized by Console
description: Troubleshooting handpiece not recognized by console due to connection, battery, or compatibility issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the device from the sterile field if actively in use and confirm an alternate tool is available. Ensure no delay in patient care.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Connect the handpiece and power on the console. Attempt activation and observe for no response, no recognition indicator, or no motor engagement.

steps.title: Check Handpiece Connection (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Fully disconnect and reconnect the handpiece. Ensure proper alignment and locking mechanism is engaged. Check for bent pins, debris, or fluid contamination.

steps.title: Verify Correct Handpiece Compatibility
steps.instructions: Check labeling and model compatibility. Ensure no cross-system components are being used.

steps.title: Inspect and Test Battery (If Applicable)
steps.instructions: Ensure battery is fully charged, swap with a known-good battery, and confirm proper seating.

steps.title: Check Console Port or Interface Module
steps.instructions: Try a different port if available and connect a known-good handpiece.

steps.title: Test with Known-Good Components
steps.instructions: Use a known-good handpiece on the same console and test the suspect handpiece on another console.

steps.title: Check for Error Indicators or Console Messages
steps.instructions: Look for error codes, fault lights, or startup self-test failures.

CCR.Complaint: OR staff reported Hall power tool handpiece not recognized by console during setup.
CCR.Cause: Fault traced to failed handpiece interface after testing with known-good console and battery.
CCR.Resolution: Removed handpiece from service and sent for vendor repair; verified backup system operational.

helpfulDetails: Any visible damage, debris, or fluid exposure
Final device status (removed from service or returned to use)

title: CONMED Hall Power Tools System - Battery Not Charging or Holding Charge
description: Troubleshooting battery charging issues, short runtime, and power failures related to charger, battery, or connection problems.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the system from active use and ensure an alternate power tool system is available.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to charge the battery and observe charging indicators, seating, and runtime performance.

steps.title: Check Charger Power Source
steps.instructions: Confirm charger is plugged into a known-good outlet and verify power indicator lights.

steps.title: Inspect Battery Seating and Contacts
steps.instructions: Ensure battery is fully seated, inspect contacts for debris or damage, and clean if necessary.

steps.title: Check Charger Indicator Behavior
steps.instructions: Observe LED indicators and compare behavior with a known-good battery if available.

steps.title: Swap with Known-Good Components
steps.instructions: Test suspect battery in a known-good charger and vice versa to isolate the failed component.

steps.title: Check for Battery Age or Usage Degradation
steps.instructions: Review battery age, usage cycles, and inspect for swelling or damage.

steps.title: Inspect for Environmental or Handling Issues
steps.instructions: Ensure proper temperature, check for contamination, and confirm correct handling practices.

CCR.Complaint: Battery not charging and losing power quickly during use.
CCR.Cause: Failed battery pack confirmed by testing in known-good charger.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced defective battery and verified proper charging and runtime.

title: CONMED Hall Power Tools Surgical Power Tool System - Motor Failure or Reduced Torque
description: Loss of power, slow rotation, or weak cutting performance due to battery, attachment, or connection-related issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the tool is in use, stop immediately and remove it from the sterile field. Provide an alternate handpiece or system.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Activate the handpiece and observe for weak rotation, intermittent operation, or stalling under load.

steps.title: Check Battery Condition (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Ensure the battery is fully charged, try a known-good battery, and inspect for age or damage.

steps.title: Inspect Battery Contacts and Seating
steps.instructions: Check for corrosion, debris, or bent contacts and ensure the battery is securely seated.

steps.title: Check Attachment/Accessory Load
steps.instructions: Test the motor without the attachment, then with it, to identify resistance from worn or binding accessories.

steps.title: Inspect for Mechanical Resistance
steps.instructions: Manually rotate the shaft if possible and check for grinding, resistance, or debris.

steps.title: Check Handpiece Temperature and Odor
steps.instructions: Assess for excessive heat or burning smell indicating internal failure.

steps.title: Test with Known-Good Components
steps.instructions: Swap in a known-good battery and attachment to isolate the issue.

CCR.Complaint: Handpiece reported to have weak power and stalled during orthopedic procedure.
CCR.Cause: Degraded battery and worn attachment increasing load on motor.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced battery and attachment; verified normal torque and operation.

helpfulDetails: Motor performance under load vs no load
Presence of heat or unusual odor
Final device status (returned to service or sent out)

title: CONMED Hall Power Tools Surgical Power Tool System - Footswitch or Activation Failure
description: Troubleshooting footswitch or activation failure causing no response, intermittent activation, or delayed motor engagement.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the device is in use, stop the procedure if safe to do so and transition to a backup system.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to activate the handpiece using the footswitch and observe for no response, intermittent activation, or delayed response.

steps.title: Check Footswitch Connection (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Ensure connector is fully seated, reconnect securely, and inspect for bent pins or debris.

steps.title: Inspect Footswitch Cable and Housing
steps.instructions: Check for cuts, kinks, exposed wiring, and ensure pedal moves freely without obstruction.

steps.title: Test with a Known Good Footswitch
steps.instructions: Swap with a working footswitch to determine if the original is faulty.

steps.title: Check Handpiece and Console Readiness
steps.instructions: Confirm handpiece connection, console power status, and absence of error messages.

steps.title: Verify Activation Mode / Settings (If Applicable)
steps.instructions: Ensure correct port usage and verify configuration settings affecting activation.

steps.title: Check for Moisture or Contamination
steps.instructions: Inspect for fluid ingress and ensure connectors are clean and dry.

CCR.Complaint: Footswitch not activating CONMED Hall Power Tool handpiece during procedure.
CCR.Cause: Faulty footswitch cable with intermittent internal break identified during swap test.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced footswitch with known good unit and verified normal activation.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (working or removed from service)

title: CONMED Hall Power Tools Surgical Power Tool System - Console Error Codes or Self-Test Failure
description: Troubleshooting console error codes and self-test failures caused by power, connection, or accessory issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot while the system is in use on a patient. Remove from service and use a backup system if needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error or Self-Test Failure
steps.instructions: Power on the console and observe the startup sequence. Note any displayed error codes or messages. Determine if failure occurs consistently during boot.

steps.title: Check AC Power Source and Power Cable (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Confirm outlet is working (test with another device). Inspect power cord for damage or loose connection. Ensure cord is fully seated in console.

steps.title: Perform Full Power Cycle Reset
steps.instructions: Turn off console. Unplug from wall for 30–60 seconds. Reconnect and restart.

steps.title: Inspect Handpiece and Cable Connections
steps.instructions: Disconnect all handpieces and attachments. Restart console with no accessories connected. Reconnect one at a time.

steps.title: Check Footswitch and External Controls
steps.instructions: Disconnect footswitch and restart console. Inspect cable and connector for damage.

steps.title: Inspect Battery/Power Module (If Applicable)
steps.instructions: Remove and reseat battery if present. Check for corrosion or poor contact.

steps.title: Observe for Hardware Indicators
steps.instructions: Listen for abnormal sounds during startup. Check for overheating, burning smell, or unusual fan behavior. Observe indicator lights.

CCR.Complaint: OR staff reported console displaying error code and failing self-test on startup.
CCR.Cause: Console failed self-test with all accessories disconnected, indicating internal fault.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent to vendor/biomed for repair evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Whether failure occurs with or without accessories
Footswitch connected or disconnected during test
Any unusual sounds, heat, or smells
Final device status (removed from service or operational)

title: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series Electrosurgical Unit (ESU) - Error: Err 384 POST HVDC to 30V failed — supply too low.
description: Troubleshooting guide for CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU Err 384, a POST failure where the HVDC supply does not reach the expected 30V level.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the ESU from patient use if Err 384 appears during startup, setup, or use. Stop use of the generator, notify clinical staff, and replace it with another approved ESU if a procedure is active or pending.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the ESU on and confirm the exact displayed error is Err 384. Note whether the error occurs every startup or intermittently and whether the unit completes POST or stops during startup.

steps.title: Power Cycle the System
steps.instructions: Turn the ESU off, disconnect AC power, wait at least 30 seconds, reconnect to a known-good hospital-grade outlet, and observe whether Err 384 returns.

steps.title: Check External Power and Basic Conditions
steps.instructions: Verify stable AC input power, inspect the power cord, check the plug and strain relief, and try another verified outlet if needed.

steps.title: Inspect the Unit for Obvious Physical Problems
steps.instructions: Inspect for liquid intrusion, impact damage, blocked vents, excessive dust, loose panels, overheating, abnormal odor, or evidence of recent drop, service, cleaning, or fluid exposure.

steps.title: Observe HVPS LED2 During POST
steps.instructions: With the unit safely positioned for bench evaluation, cycle power and observe LED2 on the high-voltage power supply during POST without touching energized components.

steps.title: Isolate the RF Amplifier Load
steps.instructions: Power down and disconnect AC power, disconnect the cable between the HVPS and RF Amplifier, reconnect power, cycle the unit on, and observe whether LED2 now illuminates during POST.

steps.title: Verify Cable Connections
steps.instructions: Inspect the HVPS-to-RF Amplifier cable and controller-to-HVPS connections for loose connectors, bent pins, damaged insulation, discoloration, poor seating, or prior repair damage.

steps.title: Verify HVPS Control Signals in Test Mode
steps.instructions: Use the appropriate service/test mode to verify that the controller is sending expected HVPS control signals and that the HVPS is receiving expected command signals.

steps.title: Check HVPS Operation
steps.instructions: Evaluate HVPS operation using the proper service procedure and test equipment. Confirm whether the HVPS can produce the expected low-level POST voltage condition.

steps.title: Determine the Likely Fault Area
steps.instructions: Use LED2 behavior, control signal findings, RF Amplifier isolation, and cable inspection results to isolate the fault to the HVPS, RF Amplifier, Controller assembly, cable connection, or intermittent internal condition.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: After correction, run repeated startups, confirm Err 384 does not return, perform required electrical safety testing, complete ESU functional/performance testing, and return the device to service only after all checks pass.

CCR.Complaint: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU reported Err 384 during startup. Unit failed POST with HVDC to 30V supply too low condition and was removed from service.
CCR.Cause: Err 384 confirmed during bench startup. External AC power and power cord checked good. Cable connections inspected. HVPS LED2 behavior checked during POST. Fault isolated to likely internal HVPS, RF Amplifier load, Controller assembly, or related cable issue depending on test findings.
CCR.Resolution: Power cycled unit and verified error behavior. Checked external power, inspected cables, observed HVPS LED2 during POST, and isolated RF Amplifier load as applicable. Unit remained Out of Service for repair/board-level evaluation, or defective assembly was replaced and ESU passed POST, safety testing, and functional output verification before return to service.

title: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series Electrosurgical Unit (ESU) - Error: Err 386 POST HVDC to 140V failed — supply too low
description: Troubleshooting guide for CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU Err 386 when the generator fails startup POST because the HVDC supply cannot reach the required 140V level.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the ESU from patient use, disconnect clinical accessories, provide staff with a known working backup generator if needed, and label the unit for Clinical Engineering evaluation.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the ESU on in a safe non-patient-care setting and confirm the exact displayed error is Err 386. Note when the error appears and whether it repeats during startup POST.

steps.title: Check External and Simple Causes First
steps.instructions: Verify the ESU is connected to a known good hospital-grade outlet, inspect the power cord and rear power entry area, and power cycle the unit.

steps.title: Remove External Accessories and Retest
steps.instructions: Disconnect the active handpiece, return electrode cable, footswitch, bipolar accessories, adapters, and specialty accessories, then power cycle the ESU again.

steps.title: Inspect the ESU for Physical or Environmental Issues
steps.instructions: Check for liquid intrusion, cleaning residue, burning smell, unusual fan noise, impact damage, loose connectors, blocked ventilation, or signs of rough transport.

steps.title: Observe HVPS LED2 During POST
steps.instructions: With the unit on the bench and following facility safety procedures, observe whether LED2 on the high-voltage power supply illuminates during POST.

steps.title: Isolate Possible RF Amplifier Loading
steps.instructions: If LED2 does not illuminate during POST, disconnect the cable between the HVPS and RF Amplifier as allowed by service procedure and retest. If LED2 illuminates after disconnection, suspect RF Amplifier loading or failure.

steps.title: Verify Internal Cable Connections
steps.instructions: Inspect and reseat accessible internal connections related to the HVPS, RF Amplifier, and Controller assembly. Check for loose connectors, damaged pins, heat discoloration, chafed wiring, arcing, or contamination.

steps.title: Verify HVPS Control Signals and Output
steps.instructions: Using the appropriate service procedure and test mode, verify that the HVPS receives proper control signals from the Controller and that HVPS output behaves as expected.

steps.title: Determine the Most Likely Fault Area
steps.instructions: Use LED2 behavior, RF Amplifier isolation results, control signals, HVPS output, and cabling condition to narrow the fault to the RF Amplifier, HVPS, Controller assembly, or wiring.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: After correction or repair, reassemble the ESU, repeat startup POST, confirm Err 386 no longer appears, and perform required ESU safety and performance testing before return to service.

CCR.Complaint: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU reported Err 386 during startup. Unit failed POST with HVDC to 140V supply too low condition and was not available for clinical use.
CCR.Cause: Err 386 was verified during POST. External accessories and power source were checked. Fault condition indicates the high-voltage DC supply could not reach the required POST level. Further isolation pointed toward possible HVPS, RF Amplifier loading, Controller signal issue, or internal cabling fault.
CCR.Resolution: Removed ESU from service and labeled Out of Service. Power cycled unit, removed accessories, inspected power cord and external condition, and verified Err 386 repeated during POST. Bench evaluation performed to observe HVPS LED2 behavior and isolate HVPS/RF Amplifier/Controller path. Unit referred for repair or component-level service as required. Final status documented as not returned to service until repair and full ESU performance testing are completed.

title: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series Electrosurgical Unit (ESU) - Error: Err 385 POST HVDC to 30V failed — supply too high
description: Troubleshooting Err 385 on the CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU when the HVDC 30V POST supply check is too high, indicating a possible HVPS regulation, controller signal, or internal connection fault.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the ESU from patient use if Err 385 is present. Do not use the generator during a procedure while this error is active. Switch to a known-good ESU if electrosurgery is needed and label the affected unit for Clinical Engineering evaluation.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the unit on in a safe non-patient-care setting and confirm the exact displayed error is Err 385. Note whether it occurs immediately at startup, during POST, intermittently, or after repeated power cycles.

steps.title: Check the Power Source First
steps.instructions: Confirm the ESU is plugged into a known-good hospital-grade outlet. Inspect the power cord and avoid questionable power strips, adapters, or damaged outlets. Power cycle the unit from a verified outlet.

steps.title: Perform a Basic External Inspection
steps.instructions: Inspect the housing, display, controls, accessory ports, output connectors, and power cord for damage, contamination, fluid intrusion, odor, heat, or signs of impact.

steps.title: Configure the Unit for Test Mode
steps.instructions: Set the ESU up for test mode according to approved service procedures. Use proper ESU test equipment and safety precautions. Do not perform internal testing unless qualified and authorized.

steps.title: Verify HVPS Control Signals From the Controller
steps.instructions: In test mode, verify HVPS control signals from the controller while activated as required by the service procedure. Check for missing, incorrect, unstable, or abnormal control signals and inspect related internal cabling.

steps.title: Verify HVPS Operation
steps.instructions: Evaluate HVPS behavior according to approved service procedure. Confirm whether the HVPS output regulates correctly during the 30V POST-related check and look for output that is too high, unstable, or not responding correctly.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Path
steps.instructions: Suspect the Controller assembly if HVPS control signals are incorrect. Suspect the HVPS if control signals are correct but output remains too high. Suspect cabling or connection issues if behavior changes when connectors are inspected or reseated.

steps.title: Verify Repair Before Returning to Service
steps.instructions: Confirm the ESU completes POST without Err 385. Perform required ESU performance and safety testing before returning the unit to clinical use.

CCR.Complaint: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU displayed Err 385 during startup and failed to complete normal POST.
CCR.Cause: Confirmed Err 385 during power-on self-test. External power source and basic external condition checked. Fault indicates HVDC 30V POST level too high, with likely HVPS regulation issue, controller signal issue, or internal connection path fault.
CCR.Resolution: Power cycled unit and retested. Removed ESU from service for bench evaluation. Verified need to check HVPS control signals and HVPS operation. Unit remained out of service pending repair of Controller assembly, HVPS, or related internal cabling as indicated.

title: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series Electrosurgical Unit (ESU) - Error: Err 387 POST HVDC to 140V Failed — Supply Too High
description: Troubleshooting guide for CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series Err 387, where POST detects the 140V HVDC supply is too high and the ESU may fail startup self-test or lock out activation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Stop using the ESU if Err 387 appears during setup, startup, or use. Notify clinical staff that the generator failed self-test and provide a backup ESU if electrosurgery is required.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the exact displayed error is Err 387. Observe whether it appears during POST, whether it is intermittent or repeatable, and whether the unit locks out operation or prevents activation.

steps.title: Power Cycle the System
steps.instructions: Turn the ESU off, disconnect it from AC power for at least 30 seconds, reconnect to a known-good hospital-grade outlet, and observe whether POST completes normally.

steps.title: Check the Power Source
steps.instructions: Plug the generator directly into a known-good wall outlet. Avoid questionable extension cords, power strips, or shared temporary power sources. Inspect the power cord and plug for damage.

steps.title: Remove External Accessories
steps.instructions: Disconnect active pencils, footswitches, return electrode cables, adapters, and active cords. Restart the ESU with only AC power connected and confirm whether Err 387 remains.

steps.title: Inspect External Condition
steps.instructions: Inspect the case, display, controls, rear panel, power inlet, and visible connectors for fluid intrusion, impact damage, contamination, missing hardware, or burning smell.

steps.title: Confirm the Fault Is Internal to the Generator
steps.instructions: If Err 387 remains after power cycling, accessory removal, and known-good AC power testing, remove the ESU from clinical use and send it for qualified bench evaluation.

steps.title: Bench Evaluation Only
steps.instructions: Qualified personnel may configure test mode, verify HVPS control signals, verify HVPS operation, and inspect internal cabling according to approved service procedures.

steps.title: Determine the Repair Path
steps.instructions: Repair or replace the HVPS if it is not regulating correctly. Replace the Controller assembly if control signals, command logic, or monitoring feedback are incorrect. Complete required post-repair testing before return to service.

CCR.Complaint: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU displayed Err 387 during startup and failed POST. Unit was not available for clinical use.
CCR.Cause: Err 387 repeated during startup after power cycle and known-good outlet check. Accessories were removed and fault remained. External causes ruled out. Fault indicates likely internal HVPS regulation, controller command, or feedback issue.
CCR.Resolution: Removed ESU from service and labeled Out of Service. Sent for bench evaluation. Test mode verification of HVPS control signals and HVPS operation required. Repair path includes Controller assembly replacement or HVPS repair/replacement based on bench findings.

title: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series Electrosurgical Unit (ESU) - Error: Err 351 Over-Power Condition
description: Troubleshooting Err 351 on the CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU when the monitor senses an over-power condition that may affect safe cutting or coagulation delivery.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the ESU from patient use if Err 351 appears during setup, activation, or clinical use. Stop use of the generator, have staff switch to another ESU if electrosurgery is still needed, and label the device for Clinical Engineering evaluation.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the exact displayed error is Err 351. Ask when the error occurred, cycle power once, and note whether the unit allows activation or locks out operation.

steps.title: Check External and Simple Causes First
steps.instructions: Inspect the power cord, plug, outlet, external connectors, active electrode, return electrode cable, footswitch, handpiece, and connected accessories. Remove questionable accessories and retest with known-good test accessories.

steps.title: Verify Settings and Load Conditions
steps.instructions: Confirm the selected mode and power setting match the reported use condition. Use an appropriate ESU analyzer and fixed load before judging output power.

steps.title: Configure for Test Mode and Retest
steps.instructions: Configure the system for test mode according to facility-approved service procedures. Cycle power, observe when Err 351 appears, and record the mode, power setting, load, and activation method.

steps.title: Measure Actual Output Power Into a Fixed Load
steps.instructions: Connect the ESU to an appropriate ESU analyzer, use a known fixed load, activate the generator in the affected mode, and compare measured output power against the selected setting and expected performance range.

steps.title: Determine Whether the Issue Is Output-Related or Sensing-Related
steps.instructions: If actual output power is high, suspect the RF output control path, HVPS, RF Amplifier, RF Transformer board, RF Output board, or Controller assembly. If actual output power is normal but Err 351 still appears, suspect Monitor or RF Controller voltage/current sensing circuits.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: If the error clears after correcting an external issue, accessory issue, setup issue, or test configuration problem, retest output using the ESU analyzer, confirm required checks pass, document the correction, and return the ESU to service only if Err 351 does not return.

CCR.Complaint: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU reported Err 351 over-power condition during activation. Unit removed from service for Clinical Engineering evaluation.
CCR.Cause: Output power measured into fixed test load was within expected range, but Err 351 returned during controlled testing. Monitor or RF Controller voltage/current sensing fault suspected.
CCR.Resolution: Removed ESU from service and labeled Out of Service. Sent for bench repair evaluation of monitor sensing, RF Controller sensing, HVPS, RF Amplifier, RF Transformer board, RF Output board, and Controller assembly.

title: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series Electrosurgical Unit (ESU) - Error: Err 352 4x Over-Power Condition
description: Troubleshooting guide for CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU Err 352, a short-duration 4x over-power condition that may indicate unsafe RF output, sensing failure, calibration issues, or internal generator failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the ESU from patient use immediately if Err 352 appears. Have staff switch to another approved ESU if electrosurgery is still needed. Do not continue using the generator with an unresolved over-power fault. Disconnect accessories as appropriate and label the device for Clinical Engineering evaluation.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the exact displayed error is Err 352. Ask when the error occurred, what mode and power setting were being used, and whether the error clears after power cycling or returns immediately.

steps.title: Check External Power and Setup First
steps.instructions: Inspect the AC power cord, plug, outlet, and power entry area for damage, looseness, heat, discoloration, or electrical stress. Verify the ESU is connected to a proper hospital-grade outlet and test from a known-good outlet if needed.

steps.title: Inspect Accessories and Connections
steps.instructions: Remove all accessories from the ESU. Inspect active electrode cables, return electrode cables, bipolar cables, footswitches, adapters, and connectors for damage, contamination, fluid intrusion, bent pins, loose fit, or overheating. Reconnect only known-good accessories for controlled testing.

steps.title: Confirm the Error Under Controlled Bench Conditions
steps.instructions: Move the ESU to a safe bench test area. Connect it to an appropriate ESU analyzer or fixed test load using approved test accessories and a known-good footswitch or handswitch. Attempt to reproduce Err 352 under controlled activation.

steps.title: Configure the System for Test Mode
steps.instructions: Configure the system for test mode according to approved service documentation and facility procedure. Do not perform internal adjustments or repairs beyond your authorization level. Prepare to measure actual RF output power into a fixed load.

steps.title: Measure Actual Output Power Into a Fixed Load
steps.instructions: Connect the ESU to the specified fixed load or ESU analyzer load. Activate the unit at controlled settings and compare measured output power to the expected output for the selected mode and setting. Watch for high output, erratic output, output spikes, or error recurrence.

steps.title: Determine Whether the Fault Is True Over-Output or Sensing-Related
steps.instructions: If measured output power is excessive, suspect RF output regulation or power control failure. If measured output power is correct but Err 352 still occurs, suspect monitor voltage/current sensing or RF Controller feedback circuitry.

steps.title: Recalibrate the System if Appropriate
steps.instructions: If the system is stable enough for calibration and your facility is authorized to perform it, recalibrate the ESU according to approved service procedure. Repeat output power testing after calibration and confirm Err 352 does not return.

steps.title: Verify Monitor and RF Controller Sensing Path
steps.instructions: If Err 352 persists, evaluate the monitor voltage/current sensing circuits and RF Controller voltage/current sensing circuits according to service procedure. Look for incorrect feedback readings, unstable sensed voltage or current, mismatches, or connector issues.

steps.title: Evaluate Likely Internal Power Section Failure
steps.instructions: If external causes, accessories, test setup issues, and calibration are ruled out, suspect internal generator failure involving the HVPS, RF Amplifier, RF Transformer board, RF Output board, or Controller assembly. Escalate to qualified repair or vendor service.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: If recalibration or correction of an external or test setup issue resolves the error, repeat functional testing, confirm output power is stable into the proper load, verify Err 352 does not recur, and complete required electrical safety and performance testing before return to service.

CCR.Complaint: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU displayed Err 352 during use. Error indicates the monitor sensed a short-duration 4x over-power condition. Unit removed from service due to possible unsafe RF output.
CCR.Cause: Err 352 confirmed during evaluation. External power, accessories, and test setup were checked. Output power testing and recalibration were attempted as applicable. Persistent fault indicates likely internal RF output regulation or voltage/current sensing failure involving monitor/RF controller feedback path, HVPS, RF amplifier, RF transformer board, RF output board, or controller assembly.
CCR.Resolution: Removed ESU from patient use and labeled Out of Service. Tested with known-good accessories and fixed load. Recalibrated/tested per service procedure as applicable. Unit requires repair or qualified bench evaluation before return to service.

title: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series Electrosurgical Unit (ESU) - Error: Err 357 Monitor Sensed Power While the Dial Power Setting Was 0 W
description: Troubleshooting Err 357 on a CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU when the monitor senses output power while the dial power setting is 0 W, creating a potential RF output safety concern.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the ESU from patient use immediately if Err 357 appears during setup, activation, or clinical use. Have staff switch to another approved ESU if electrosurgery is still needed. Do not continue using the generator with an unresolved sensed-power fault.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the unit on in a safe, non-patient-use setting and confirm the exact displayed error is Err 357. Ask when the error occurred and note whether the unit alarms, disables output, fails POST, or faults intermittently.

steps.title: Check the Power Setting and Front Panel Controls
steps.instructions: Confirm the power setting is actually set to 0 W. Inspect the power adjustment controls for sticking, contamination, damage, or erratic behavior. Return the control to 0 W, power cycle the unit, and check whether the error returns.

steps.title: Remove External Accessories
steps.instructions: Disconnect footswitches, handpieces, active pencils, bipolar accessories, dispersive electrode cables, and output cables. Inspect accessories for fluid contamination, stuck activation buttons, damaged insulation, or bent pins. Retest with accessories removed.

steps.title: Inspect External Connections and Physical Condition
steps.instructions: Inspect ESU output ports, power cord, plug, and AC connection. Verify the unit is connected to a properly grounded hospital-grade outlet. Look for fluid intrusion, burns, odor, overheating, impact damage, or contamination.

steps.title: Power Cycle and Recheck
steps.instructions: Turn the ESU off, disconnect AC power briefly, reconnect power, and restart the unit. Confirm whether Err 357 returns during POST, idle state, or activation testing.

steps.title: Perform Bench Testing Only If Qualified and Equipped
steps.instructions: Move the ESU to a controlled bench environment. Use an approved ESU analyzer or fixed test load. Measure output power while confirming whether any power is present when the commanded setting is 0 W.

steps.title: Recalibrate if Appropriate
steps.instructions: If authorized and equipped, recalibrate the system according to approved service procedures. Repeat output testing into a fixed load and confirm Err 357 does not return.

steps.title: Evaluate Likely Internal Failure Areas
steps.instructions: If the error persists after external checks, accessory isolation, power cycling, and calibration, suspect internal failure involving monitor sensing, RF Controller sensing, HVPS, RF Amplifier, RF Transformer board, RF Output board, or Controller assembly.

CCR.Complaint: User reported CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU displayed Err 357. Error indicates monitor sensed output power while dial power setting was 0 W. Unit removed from patient use for evaluation.
CCR.Cause: Confirmed Err 357 during evaluation. Power setting, accessories, external connections, and power source were checked. Fault persisted with accessories removed, indicating likely internal output sensing, RF control, HVPS, RF amplifier, RF output, or controller-related failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed ESU from service and labeled Out of Service. Unit sent for repair or advanced bench evaluation. Safety and performance testing required before return to service.

title: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series Electrosurgical Unit (ESU) - Error: Err 359 Peak Voltage Above LAP Limit
description: Troubleshooting Err 359 when the CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU senses peak voltages above the LAP limit, indicating a possible unsafe RF output, sensing, accessory, or internal control issue.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the ESU from patient use immediately. Do not continue surgical use after Err 359 appears. Provide clinical staff with a known-good backup ESU if the unit was needed for a procedure. Label the unit clearly if the error repeats or cannot be immediately resolved.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the ESU on and confirm whether Err 359 is displayed. Determine whether the error occurs during startup, during activation, only in a specific mode, only with a specific accessory, or intermittently during use. Ask what accessories were connected when the error occurred.

steps.title: Check the Power Source and Basic External Setup
steps.instructions: Plug the ESU directly into a known-good hospital-grade outlet. Avoid power strips, extension cords, loose receptacles, or shared temporary power sources. Inspect the power cord for damage, loose pins, cuts, heat marks, or strain relief issues.

steps.title: Inspect the ESU Exterior and Front Panel
steps.instructions: Inspect the generator for fluid intrusion, physical damage, burn marks, cracked housing, damaged output jacks, loose controls, or evidence of dropped equipment. Verify the power setting controls respond normally and are not stuck, damaged, or erratic.

steps.title: Remove Accessories and Retest
steps.instructions: Disconnect all patient cables, active accessories, return electrode cables, footswitches, adapters, and specialty accessories. Power cycle the unit and observe whether Err 359 appears with nothing connected. Reconnect accessories one at a time if the error clears.

steps.title: Check Active Accessories and Cables
steps.instructions: Inspect the active electrode cable, handpiece, laparoscopic accessory, or specialty cable used during the event. Look for cracked insulation, exposed conductor, burned connectors, loose plug fit, contamination, wrong accessory type, or reusable accessory damage. Swap with a known-good compatible accessory and retest.

steps.title: Check Return Electrode Cable and REM Setup
steps.instructions: Inspect the patient return electrode cable and connector. Verify the return cable is compatible and fully seated. Check for damaged pins, contamination, loose fit, or cable strain. Test with a known-good return cable and correct test setup if applicable.

steps.title: Verify Mode and Power Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm the reported mode and settings used when Err 359 occurred. Check whether the error appears only in a specific mode, such as cut, coag, blend, bipolar, or LAP-related output mode. Reduce settings to a safe test level during bench evaluation.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled Bench Test With Proper Load
steps.instructions: Remove the ESU from clinical use and test it on the bench using an ESU analyzer or approved fixed load setup. Configure the system for test mode if appropriate. Measure output power into a fixed load and compare actual output behavior to expected performance.

steps.title: Recalibrate the System if Qualified
steps.instructions: If authorized and equipped, recalibrate the system following approved service procedures. After calibration, repeat output testing into the proper fixed load and confirm whether Err 359 clears and output remains stable.

steps.title: Verify Sensing and Output Control Path
steps.instructions: If Err 359 persists after external checks, accessory swaps, proper load testing, and calibration, evaluate service-level fault paths including monitor voltage/current sensing circuits, RF Controller sensing circuits, HVPS, RF Amplifier, RF Transformer board, RF Output board, and Controller assembly.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: If the error clears after correcting an external issue, replacing a damaged accessory, verifying output into a proper load, or completing calibration, document the finding. Perform operational testing, confirm output performance is acceptable, clean the unit, and return it to service according to facility policy.

CCR.Complaint: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU reported Err 359 during activation/use. Error indicates monitor sensed peak voltages above the LAP limit.
CCR.Cause: Err 359 confirmed during evaluation. External power, accessories, active cable, return cable, and output setup checked. Fault persisted during bench testing, indicating likely internal voltage/current sensing, HVPS, RF amplifier, RF output, RF transformer, or controller-related failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed ESU from service and labeled Out of Service. Unit requires service-level repair or vendor evaluation. Not returned to clinical use due to unresolved peak-voltage fault.

title: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series Electrosurgical Unit (ESU) - Error: Err 383 RF current sensed at an unselected output
description: Troubleshooting guide for Err 383 on the CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU when RF current is detected at an output that was not selected, indicating a possible accessory, connector, output relay, Output assembly, or Controller assembly fault.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Stop use of the generator immediately if Err 383 appears during setup, activation, testing, or clinical use. Switch to a known working backup ESU if electrosurgery is still needed. Disconnect accessories and label the unit for Clinical Engineering evaluation if the error repeats.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the exact displayed error is Err 383. Document when it occurs, which output was selected, which mode was in use, and whether the error appears at startup, during activation, in a specific mode, or intermittently.

steps.title: Inspect the External Setup First
steps.instructions: Check the ESU power cord, wall outlet, equipotential grounding connection if used, and all output connectors. Look for bent contacts, contamination, fluid intrusion, burn marks, loose connectors, foreign material, or signs of arcing.

steps.title: Remove Accessories and Retest
steps.instructions: Disconnect all accessories from all outputs, cycle power, and check whether Err 383 returns with nothing connected. If the error clears, reconnect accessories one at a time and remove any accessory or cable that causes the error to return.

steps.title: Check for Accessory or Cable Cross-Connection Issues
steps.instructions: Verify accessories are connected to the correct output ports. Inspect cables and adapters for damage, exposed conductors, melted areas, arcing, or incorrect use. Test with known good accessories if available.

steps.title: Verify Output Selection and Activation Method
steps.instructions: Confirm the selected mode and output match the accessory being tested. Check whether the error occurs only with hand activation, footswitch activation, a specific mode, a specific power setting, or a specific output connector.

steps.title: Bench Test with Fixed Loads
steps.instructions: Remove the device from clinical use and bench test using appropriate ESU test equipment and fixed loads. Activate each output one at a time and monitor all outputs for RF current. Current should only be detected at the selected output.

steps.title: Identify Whether the Fault Is Output-Specific
steps.instructions: Document which selected output causes the error and which unselected output shows current or triggers the fault. Compare behavior across monopolar and bipolar outputs to help identify a possible stuck relay, Output assembly issue, sensing issue, or Controller assembly fault.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: If the problem is traced to an external accessory, damaged cable, contaminated connector, or incorrect setup, remove the faulty item from service, retest the ESU with known good accessories and proper loads, and complete required performance and safety checks before return to service.

CCR.Complaint: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU reported Err 383. Staff reported RF current sensed at an unselected output during activation/testing.
CCR.Cause: Verified Err 383 condition. External accessories, output cables, activation method, and connector condition checked. Fixed-load testing indicated possible RF current detection on an output that was not selected.
CCR.Resolution: Removed ESU from service and labeled Out of Service. Unit requires repair or further bench evaluation of output switching, output sensing, Output assembly, and Controller assembly before return to service.

title: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series Electrosurgical Unit (ESU) - Error: Err 350 Monitor Output Channel Current Sensor Failure
description: Troubleshooting guide for Err 350 on the CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU, indicating a monitor output channel current sensor failure that may affect RF output monitoring accuracy.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the ESU from patient use if Err 350 appears during setup, activation, or procedure use. Notify clinical staff that the generator has reported an internal RF output monitoring fault. Do not allow the unit to be used for cutting, coagulation, bipolar, or specialty output modes until evaluated. Replace it with a known-good ESU if the case is active.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the unit on in a safe, non-patient-care setting and confirm the exact displayed error is Err 350. Determine whether the error occurs during startup, after power cycling, during activation, only under load, or intermittently.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Basic External Conditions
steps.instructions: Plug the ESU into a known-good hospital-grade outlet. Avoid power strips or questionable outlets during testing. Inspect the power cord, plug, strain relief, and ground pin. Confirm the unit powers on normally aside from the reported error.

steps.title: Inspect External Connections and Accessories
steps.instructions: Remove all patient cables, pencils, bipolar cords, return electrode cables, adapters, and accessories. Inspect the output receptacles for bent contacts, contamination, damage, fluid intrusion, or debris. Reconnect only known-good test accessories as needed for bench testing.

steps.title: Confirm the Test Setup Is Correct
steps.instructions: Verify that the ESU analyzer or fixed load is appropriate for the output mode being tested. Confirm the load is connected to the correct active and return or bipolar output path. Confirm the selected power setting and output mode match the intended test.

steps.title: Configure the System for Test Mode and Cycle Power
steps.instructions: Configure the ESU for the appropriate service or test mode according to approved facility or service procedures. Cycle power after entering the required test configuration. Observe whether Err 350 clears, returns immediately, or returns during activation.

steps.title: Test Output Into a Fixed Load
steps.instructions: Connect the ESU to an appropriate ESU analyzer or fixed load. Select a controlled output mode and constant power setting. Activate the unit briefly while observing output behavior. Compare displayed or measured output current against expected analyzer behavior.

steps.title: Compare Output Channel Current With Output Current
steps.instructions: Use approved service diagnostic tools, such as DACview if available and authorized. Compare the output channel current sensor reading with actual output current while activating at constant power into a fixed load. Look for missing, frozen, incorrect, unstable, or mismatched current sensor values.

steps.title: Isolate Accessory, Setup, or Device Failure
steps.instructions: If the error clears with known-good accessories and correct load setup, document the faulty accessory or setup condition. If the error repeats with known-good loads and controlled test conditions, treat the fault as device-related and remove the unit from service.

CCR.Complaint: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU reported Err 350. Staff stated unit alarmed or failed during use or setup. Error indicates monitor output channel current sensor failure.
CCR.Cause: Verified Err 350 during evaluation. External power, accessories, output connections, and test load setup checked. Error persisted under controlled test conditions, indicating likely internal RF output current sensing or output board pathway failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed ESU from service and labeled Out of Service. Unit requires bench repair or vendor evaluation of RF Amplifier, RF Transformer board, RF Output board, Controller assembly, and current sensing circuitry before return to service.

title: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series Electrosurgical Unit (ESU) - Error: Err 373 Damping circuit failed POST — damping not detected
description: Troubleshooting guide for Err 373 on a CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU when the generator fails POST because the damping circuit is not detected, indicating a possible RF Amplifier damping circuit or Controller assembly fault.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the ESU from patient use if Err 373 appears during startup, setup, or clinical use. Stop use of the generator, notify clinical staff that the ESU failed self-test, provide a replacement ESU if needed, and do not continue clinical use after a POST failure.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the unit on and confirm the displayed error is Err 373. Record whether the error occurs during POST or after activation, whether the generator completes startup, and whether any other errors appear.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Basic Setup
steps.instructions: Verify the ESU is connected to a known-good hospital-grade outlet. Confirm the power cord is fully seated and undamaged, and check for loose connections, damaged plugs, heat, smell, or intermittent power.

steps.title: Inspect the Unit Externally
steps.instructions: Inspect the front panel, rear panel, output ports, and accessory receptacles for fluid intrusion, cracked housing, impact damage, debris, bent pins, or contamination. Disconnect external accessories and retry startup.

steps.title: Power Cycle and Recheck
steps.instructions: Turn the ESU off, disconnect power briefly, reconnect power, and restart the unit. Observe whether Err 373 returns during POST.

steps.title: Isolate From User Setup
steps.instructions: Confirm the error occurs without patient accessories connected. Verify the issue is not tied to one handpiece, footswitch, return electrode cable, or adapter.

steps.title: Configure for Bench Evaluation or Test Mode
steps.instructions: Remove the unit from clinical use and move it to a controlled bench area. Configure the system for test mode only if trained, authorized, and equipped to do so. Use a proper ESU analyzer and load before activation testing.

steps.title: Verify Damping-Related Operation During Coag Modes
steps.instructions: In test mode, activate the unit in Spray Coag or Standard Coag using the proper fixed load/test setup. Verify that the RFDAMP_DR signal switches high immediately before each RF drive pulse and switches low after each RF drive pulse.

steps.title: Inspect Internal Connections Only at an Appropriate Service Level
steps.instructions: If authorized, inspect accessible internal harnesses and connectors related to the RF Amplifier and Controller assemblies. Look for loose connectors, damaged wiring, poor seating, contamination, or signs of overheating.

steps.title: Determine Whether the Fault Is Device-Related
steps.instructions: If Err 373 remains after power, accessory, setup, and connection checks, treat the issue as an internal ESU failure. Suspect the RF Amplifier damping circuitry or Controller assembly.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: Repeat startup testing, confirm Err 373 no longer appears, perform required ESU performance testing, perform electrical safety testing if required, verify RF output with an approved analyzer and load, document the final status, and return the device to service only after all required checks pass.

CCR.Complaint: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU reported Err 373 during startup. Unit failed POST with damping circuit not detected and was unavailable for clinical use.
CCR.Cause: Err 373 confirmed during power-on testing. External accessories disconnected and power source checked. Error persisted, indicating likely internal damping circuit, RF Amplifier, or Controller assembly failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed ESU from service and labeled Out of Service. Sent for bench repair/evaluation. RF Amplifier damping circuitry and Controller assembly to be evaluated before return to service.

title: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series Electrosurgical Unit (ESU) - Error: Err 374 Damping circuit failed POST — damping stuck on
description: Troubleshooting guide for Err 374 on a CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU when the damping circuit fails POST because damping is detected as stuck on, preventing reliable RF output operation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the ESU from patient use if Err 374 appears during startup, setup, or clinical use. Stop use of the generator, notify clinical staff, provide a known working backup ESU if needed, and do not continue using the generator if the error repeats.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the unit on in a safe non-patient-care setting. Confirm the displayed error is Err 374 and document whether it occurs during POST, after power-up, or during activation testing.

steps.title: Cycle Power and Recheck
steps.instructions: Turn the ESU off, disconnect it from AC power briefly, reconnect power, and restart the unit. Observe whether Err 374 returns during POST.

steps.title: Check Power Source and External Setup
steps.instructions: Verify the ESU is connected to a proper hospital-grade outlet. Inspect the power cord and confirm the unit is not connected through a questionable power strip or damaged outlet. Disconnect external accessories during startup if appropriate.

steps.title: Inspect the Unit Externally
steps.instructions: Check the housing, power cord, output ports, and visible condition for damage, contamination, overheating, fluid intrusion, burn marks, abnormal odor, or evidence of impact.

steps.title: Verify Internal Cable Connections During Bench Evaluation
steps.instructions: Remove the unit from clinical use and perform bench evaluation according to facility policy. With power disconnected, qualified personnel may verify internal cables related to the RF Amplifier and Controller assemblies are fully seated and not damaged.

steps.title: Configure for Test Mode if Available and Authorized
steps.instructions: Configure the ESU for test mode only if trained and authorized. Attempt to reproduce the fault under controlled bench conditions and determine whether it appears during POST or activation testing.

steps.title: Check Damping Control Behavior During Spray or Standard Coag
steps.instructions: Using proper test equipment and service procedures, check the RFDAMP_DR signal during Spray or Standard Coag activation. Verify whether the damping drive signal changes state correctly during RF activation.

steps.title: Isolate the Likely Failure Area
steps.instructions: If cabling is secure and damping control remains abnormal, suspect the RF Amplifier assembly, damping circuitry, possible Q1 failure, Controller assembly, or related internal control path.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: After any corrective action, run startup successfully, complete functional testing, verify output operation using approved ESU test equipment, complete electrical safety testing if required, and return the device to service only after all required checks pass.

CCR.Complaint: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU reported Err 374 during startup. Error indicates damping circuit failed POST with damping detected stuck on. Unit removed from patient use.
CCR.Cause: Err 374 confirmed during POST. External power and setup checked. No accessory-related cause identified. Internal cable connections require verification. Persistent fault indicates likely RF Amplifier damping circuit failure, possible Q1 failure, or Controller assembly issue.
CCR.Resolution: Removed ESU from service and labeled Out of Service. Verified reported Err 374 condition. Checked external power, power cord, output ports, and visible condition. Unit requires bench evaluation of internal cabling, RFDAMP_DR signal, RF Amplifier assembly, and Controller assembly before return to service.

title: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series Electrosurgical Unit (ESU) - Error: Err 381 No tone feedback during POST
description: Troubleshooting guide for Err 381 on a CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU when the generator does not detect tone feedback during POST, indicating a possible speaker, volume, cable, back panel, controller, or tone feedback circuit issue.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the ESU from patient use if Err 381 appears during startup or if the unit does not complete POST normally. Do not allow the generator to be used for a procedure while the error is present. Provide a known working ESU if surgical support is needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the unit on in a safe, non-patient-use setting. Confirm that Err 381 is displayed. Listen for expected tones during POST and note whether tones are absent, faint, distorted, intermittent, or normal.

steps.title: Check the Tone Volume Control
steps.instructions: Check the back-panel tone volume control. Increase the volume setting, power cycle the ESU, and listen again during POST. If the error clears, verify normal startup and complete required functional checks.

steps.title: Check External Setup and Basic Conditions
steps.instructions: Confirm the ESU is connected to a proper power source. Inspect for impact damage, fluid intrusion, contamination, loose hardware, or anything blocking or muffling the tone output.

steps.title: Listen for Tone Output During Safe Activation Checks
steps.instructions: Only perform activation checks outside patient use and according to facility policy. Use appropriate ESU test equipment and listen for activation or alarm tones to determine whether tone failure is absent, intermittent, faint, or limited to POST.

steps.title: Check Accessible Cable Connections
steps.instructions: Remove the device from service before opening any covers. Follow facility and manufacturer precautions. Check accessible cable connections related to the back panel, speaker or tone circuit, and controller connections.

steps.title: Escalate to Bench-Level Tone Circuit Evaluation
steps.instructions: If basic checks do not resolve the error, configure the system for test mode if available and authorized. Check whether the controller is sending the tone drive signal and whether TONE_MON feedback returns to the controller.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: Confirm POST completes successfully, tones are audible, and required operational and safety checks pass before returning the ESU to service.

CCR.Complaint: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU displayed Err 381 during POST. Staff reported no audible tone feedback during startup.
CCR.Cause: Confirmed Err 381 during startup. Back-panel tone volume checked and increased. External condition inspected. Accessible tone and back panel cabling checked. Error persisted, indicating likely internal tone drive, tone feedback, Back Panel assembly, or Controller assembly fault.
CCR.Resolution: Removed ESU from service and labeled Out of Service for bench evaluation. Further troubleshooting needed in test mode to verify tone drive signal, TONE_MON feedback, Controller assembly, and Back Panel assembly.

title: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series Electrosurgical Unit (ESU) - Error: Err 382 Activation without a tone
description: Troubleshooting guide for Err 382 on a CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series electrosurgical unit when the ESU activates without the required audible tone, creating a safety concern during RF energy delivery.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the ESU from patient use if Err 382 is active or if activation occurs without an audible tone. Notify clinical staff, provide a known working backup ESU if needed, and do not continue clinical use until the tone issue is corrected and verified.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the displayed error is Err 382. Using a safe test setup, activate Cut and Coag and listen for the activation tone. Note whether the tone is absent, weak, intermittent, distorted, or delayed.

steps.title: Check the Tone Volume Setting
steps.instructions: Locate the back-panel tone volume control, increase the volume to a clearly audible level, and retest activation. If the tone returns and the error does not repeat, document the adjustment and complete operational checks.

steps.title: Inspect the Speaker Opening and External Condition
steps.instructions: Inspect the rear panel and speaker area for blockage, tape, debris, contamination, fluid residue, or physical damage that could muffle or prevent audible tone output.

steps.title: Confirm the Issue Is Not Accessory-Related
steps.instructions: Test activation using a known good handpiece or footswitch. Confirm the ESU recognizes activation normally and determine whether RF output activates while the audible tone remains missing.

steps.title: Cycle Power and Retest
steps.instructions: Turn the ESU off, disconnect it from power briefly, reconnect it to a known good hospital-grade outlet, allow POST to complete, and retest activation tone using a safe setup.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Basic Electrical Condition
steps.instructions: Verify the unit is connected to a known good outlet, inspect the power cord and plug, and confirm there are no additional power-related or POST errors.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Pattern
steps.instructions: Determine whether the tone failure occurs during activation only or during both POST and activation. Identify whether Cut, Coag, or both modes are affected and whether the tone is absent, weak, intermittent, or distorted.

steps.title: Escalate for Qualified Bench Evaluation
steps.instructions: If the error persists after external checks, volume adjustment, power cycling, and accessory checks, remove the unit from service. Qualified service personnel may evaluate internal cabling, tone drive, TONE_MON feedback, Controller assembly, Back Panel assembly, speaker path, and related circuitry.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: Verify the activation tone is clearly audible, confirm Err 382 does not return after multiple activations, perform required operational checks, complete electrical safety testing if required, and return the unit to service only when it passes all required checks.

CCR.Complaint: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU reported Err 382 Activation without a tone. Staff reported unit activated but no audible activation tone was heard during use.
CCR.Cause: Verified activation tone failure during testing. Tone volume, power source, speaker opening, external condition, and activation accessories were checked. Error persisted, indicating likely internal tone drive, tone feedback, Controller assembly, Back Panel assembly, speaker path, or cabling issue.
CCR.Resolution: Removed ESU from service and labeled Out of Service. Sent unit for repair/bench evaluation due to unresolved Err 382 activation-without-tone condition. Unit not returned to clinical use.

title: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series Electrosurgical Unit (ESU) - Error: Err 114 Display or keyboard SPI communication failure
description: Troubleshooting guide for Err 114 on a CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series electrosurgical unit when display or keyboard SPI communication fails, causing possible control panel, display, or user input problems.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the ESU from patient use if Err 114 is active or if the display, keyboard, or front panel controls are unreliable. Provide a known working backup ESU if electrosurgery is needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the unit on in a safe non-patient-use setting and confirm the displayed error is Err 114. Check whether the error occurs during startup, POST, setup, or after touching the front panel controls.

steps.title: Cycle System Power
steps.instructions: Turn the ESU off, disconnect power briefly, reconnect power, and restart the unit without pressing keys during startup. If the error clears, perform a full functional check before returning the unit to service.

steps.title: Check the Power Source and Startup Conditions
steps.instructions: Verify the ESU is connected to a known good hospital-grade outlet. Confirm the power cord is fully seated and undamaged, then restart the unit and check whether Err 114 returns.

steps.title: Inspect the Front Panel and Display Area
steps.instructions: Inspect the display and keyboard area for physical damage, cracked overlay, liquid intrusion, sticky buttons, stuck keys, or cleaning solution entry.

steps.title: Remove External Accessories and Retest
steps.instructions: Disconnect footswitches, active handpieces, return electrode cables, bipolar accessories, and smoke evacuator activation cables if connected. Retest the unit with only the power cord connected.

steps.title: Verify Keyboard and Display Function
steps.instructions: If the ESU starts without the error, confirm buttons respond correctly, display information changes properly, and displayed settings match selected mode and output settings.

steps.title: Inspect Accessible Cable Connections During Bench Evaluation
steps.instructions: With the ESU removed from service, qualified personnel should inspect the cable path between the Display assembly and Controller assembly for loose connectors, damaged harnesses, pinched routing, corrosion, or contamination.

steps.title: Isolate the Likely Display, Cable, or Controller Failure
steps.instructions: If Err 114 persists after external checks, treat the fault as an internal communication-path problem involving the Display-to-Controller cable, Display assembly, or Controller assembly.

steps.title: Perform Advanced Signal Verification If Qualified
steps.instructions: During qualified bench service, verify SPI communication activity if equipped and authorized. Signals of interest may include SPI_SCK, SPI_MOSI, and SPI_MISO.

CCR.Complaint: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU displayed Err 114 display or keyboard SPI communication failure during startup. Staff reported display/control panel communication issue.
CCR.Cause: Confirmed Err 114 on startup. Power source, external accessories, front panel condition, and keyboard/display response were checked. Fault persisted, indicating likely internal display-to-controller communication issue involving cable, Display assembly, or Controller assembly.
CCR.Resolution: Removed ESU from service and labeled Out of Service. Sent for bench evaluation/repair of Display-to-Controller cable, Display assembly, or Controller assembly. Unit not returned to service pending repair and functional verification.
