title: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series Electrosurgical Unit (ESU) - Error: Err 117 5.0 volt too low
description: Troubleshooting guide for Err 117 on a CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series electrosurgical unit when the generator detects a low 5.0 V supply condition affecting controller or logic circuit operation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the ESU from patient use if Err 117 appears during setup, startup, or clinical use. Provide a known-good replacement ESU before the procedure continues.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the ESU on in a safe, non-patient-care setting and confirm the exact displayed error is Err 117. Observe whether it appears during startup, POST, intermittently, or during activation.

steps.title: Check the AC Power Source First
steps.instructions: Verify the ESU is connected to a known-good hospital-grade outlet. Inspect the power cord and confirm the outlet is not switched, damaged, or overloaded.

steps.title: Inspect the Power Cord and External Condition
steps.instructions: Inspect the power entry area, rear panel, fuse holder, cord strain relief, and exterior housing for damage, liquid intrusion, discoloration, impact, odor, or overheating.

steps.title: Power Cycle and Recheck the Error
steps.instructions: Turn the ESU off, disconnect it from AC power briefly, reconnect it to a known-good outlet, and power it back on to see whether Err 117 returns.

steps.title: Remove Accessories and Retest
steps.instructions: Disconnect handpieces, footswitches, return electrode cables, bipolar cables, and other accessories. Power cycle the generator with no accessories connected and observe whether Err 117 remains.

steps.title: Check Basic Front Panel Behavior
steps.instructions: Observe display operation, button response, startup tones, resets, freezes, dimming, or shutdown behavior to help document whether the low-voltage logic supply may be affecting control electronics.

steps.title: Escalate to Bench Evaluation
steps.instructions: If external causes are ruled out, verify the low-voltage power supply output and Controller ADC reference voltages. Repair or replace the power supply or Controller assembly as needed, then complete required functional and safety testing.

CCR.Complaint: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU displayed Err 117, 5.0 volt too low, during startup. Unit removed from service due to power supply-related system fault.
CCR.Cause: Verified Err 117 was repeatable. External AC outlet, power cord, accessories, and basic setup were checked. Fault indicates low 5.0 V supply condition or Controller voltage monitoring issue.
CCR.Resolution: Removed ESU from clinical use and labeled Out of Service. Unit requires bench evaluation of low-voltage power supply output and Controller ADC reference voltages. Repair to include power supply replacement if output is out of limits or Controller assembly replacement if supply voltage is normal and fault persists.

title: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series Electrosurgical Unit (ESU) - Error: Err 118 5.0 volt too high
description: Troubleshooting guide for Err 118 on the CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU when the generator detects the internal 5.0 V supply as too high, which may prevent normal startup or safe operation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the ESU from patient use if Err 118 is displayed or if the generator does not operate normally. Stop use of the ESU, provide a known-good replacement generator if needed, and do not continue using the unit with an unresolved voltage-related error.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the unit on and confirm the exact displayed error is Err 118. Note whether the error appears immediately at startup, during POST, intermittently, or after the unit warms up.

steps.title: Translate the Error Into a Real-World Condition
steps.instructions: Treat Err 118 as an internal 5.0 V over-limit condition involving the low-voltage power supply, Controller voltage monitoring circuit, ADC reference voltage, or internal power regulation.

steps.title: Check the Power Source First
steps.instructions: Verify the ESU is connected to a known-good hospital-grade outlet. Inspect the power cord, plug, strain relief, and inlet for damage, looseness, burning, or contamination.

steps.title: Inspect the Exterior Condition
steps.instructions: Check the ESU for signs of fluid intrusion, impact damage, cracked housing, missing screws, damaged controls, or contamination. Confirm the unit has not recently been dropped, cleaned excessively, or exposed to liquid.

steps.title: Cycle Power and Recheck
steps.instructions: Turn the ESU off, disconnect power for a short period, reconnect power, and restart the unit. Confirm whether Err 118 returns.

steps.title: Remove Accessories From the Equation
steps.instructions: Disconnect handpieces, footswitches, patient return electrode cables, bipolar accessories, and external accessories. Power the unit on with only the power cord connected and observe whether Err 118 still appears.

steps.title: Check for Configuration or Use-Related Clues
steps.instructions: Ask staff what happened before the error appeared. Determine whether the error occurred after cleaning, transport, storage, a power event, or a previous repair.

steps.title: Bench Evaluate the Low-Voltage Supply
steps.instructions: Remove the unit from clinical use and evaluate it on the bench. Check the output of the low-voltage power supply according to service procedure and facility safety practices.

steps.title: Verify Controller Reference Voltage
steps.instructions: If the low-voltage power supply output appears within limits, verify the ADC reference voltages on the Controller and confirm the reference voltage is approximately 2.5 V where applicable.

steps.title: Isolate Power Supply Versus Controller
steps.instructions: If the 5.0 V supply output is out of limits, suspect the low-voltage power supply. If the 5.0 V supply output is within limits but Err 118 remains, suspect the Controller assembly or voltage sensing/reference circuit.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: Confirm Err 118 no longer appears, verify the ESU completes startup normally, perform required operational and safety testing, and return the device to service only after successful testing.

CCR.Complaint: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU reported with Err 118 5.0 volt too high. Unit would not complete normal operation and was removed from clinical use.
CCR.Cause: Confirmed Err 118 during startup/bench check. External power source, power cord, accessories, and visible physical condition checked. Error indicates internal 5.0 V supply over-limit condition or Controller voltage sensing/reference issue.
CCR.Resolution: Removed unit from service. Bench evaluation performed. Low-voltage power supply output and Controller ADC reference voltage require verification. If 5.0 V supply is out of limits, replace low-voltage power supply. If supply is within limits and fault persists, replace Controller assembly. Final return to service requires successful startup, operational verification, and required safety testing.

title: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series Electrosurgical Unit (ESU) - Error: Err 136 A.R.M.™ circuit dropped below 0.4 V.
description: Troubleshooting guide for CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU Err 136 when the A.R.M.™ circuit voltage drops below 0.4 V, indicating a possible return electrode monitoring signal, Output assembly, signal path, or Controller assembly issue.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the ESU from patient use if Err 136 appears. Have clinical staff switch to another approved ESU if electrosurgery is still needed. Do not attempt repeated activation on a patient while the error is unresolved.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the unit on in a safe non-patient-use setting and confirm the exact displayed error is Err 136. Note whether it appears during startup, POST, return electrode connection, activation, or intermittent movement.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Basic Setup
steps.instructions: Verify the ESU is connected to a known-good hospital-grade outlet. Inspect the power cord for damage or loose fit. Power-cycle the unit once and avoid repeated resets if the error returns.

steps.title: Check External Accessories and Return Electrode Setup
steps.instructions: Remove connected pencils, footswitches, return electrodes, adapters, and active cables. Inspect the return electrode connector area for damage, contamination, fluid intrusion, debris, or bent contacts. Test with known-good accessories if needed.

steps.title: Bench Evaluate the A.R.M.™ Signal Path
steps.instructions: Verify the VARM signal entering the Controller assembly according to approved service procedures. If VARM entering the Controller is faulty, suspect the Output assembly or signal path. If VARM appears correct, suspect the Controller assembly.

steps.title: Determine Corrective Action
steps.instructions: Repair or replace the Output assembly if the VARM signal entering the Controller is faulty. Replace the Controller assembly if VARM is correct entering the Controller. Perform functional testing, ESU performance verification, and safety testing before return to service.

CCR.Complaint: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU displayed Err 136 A.R.M.™ circuit voltage low error and was removed from service.
CCR.Cause: Err 136 confirmed during bench evaluation. External power, accessories, return electrode setup, and connector condition were checked. Fault persisted, indicating likely internal A.R.M.™ signal path, Output assembly, or Controller assembly failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed unit from service and labeled Out of Service. Verified VARM signal path per service procedure. Unit requires Output assembly or Controller assembly repair based on VARM findings, followed by functional testing, ESU performance verification, and safety testing before return to service.

title: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series Electrosurgical Unit (ESU) - Error: Err 144 RF Controller and Monitor power levels do not match.
description: Troubleshooting Err 144 on a CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU when the RF Controller and Monitor power readings do not match, which may indicate unsafe or inaccurate RF output monitoring.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the ESU from patient use if Err 144 appears during startup, setup, activation, or clinical use. Notify clinical staff, provide a replacement ESU if needed, and do not continue using the generator until the fault is resolved and output performance is verified.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the ESU on and confirm whether Err 144 is displayed. Determine whether the error occurs at startup, during POST, during activation, in a specific mode, or intermittently. Record the selected mode, power setting, accessory, and output jack involved.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Basic External Conditions
steps.instructions: Verify the ESU is connected to a known-good hospital-grade outlet. Confirm the power cord is fully seated and undamaged. Check for obvious signs of damage, overheating, liquid intrusion, or contamination. Power cycle the unit and observe whether Err 144 returns.

steps.title: Check ESU Accessories and Output Connections
steps.instructions: Remove all accessories from the ESU. Inspect monopolar pencils, bipolar forceps, footswitches, return electrode cables, adapters, and output connectors for damage or contamination. Reconnect only the minimum required accessories for controlled testing and use known-good accessories.

steps.title: Confirm the Error Is Not Mode- or Accessory-Specific
steps.instructions: Use appropriate ESU test equipment and a controlled load. Check whether Err 144 appears in Cut, Blend, Coag, Spray, Bipolar, or all modes. Test one output configuration at a time and record whether the fault follows a specific mode, output connector, power range, or accessory configuration.

steps.title: Verify Displayed Power Versus Actual Output Power
steps.instructions: Connect the ESU to an approved ESU analyzer with the correct load for the mode being tested. Set a controlled power setting, activate according to safe test procedure, and compare measured output power to the displayed power setting.

steps.title: Evaluate Monitor Feedback
steps.instructions: Use available service diagnostics, such as DACview if applicable, to observe Monitor readings during controlled activation. Compare Monitor voltage, current, and calculated power values against expected behavior and measured ESU analyzer output.

steps.title: Compare RF Controller and Monitor Sensing Channels
steps.instructions: During controlled bench testing, compare voltage and current sensing channels for both the RF Controller and Monitor. Check whether the mismatch follows a specific mode, output connector, power range, voltage feedback channel, current feedback channel, or general controller and monitor disagreement.

steps.title: Inspect External Signs Before Considering Internal Failure
steps.instructions: Check output connectors and accessory sockets for damage, looseness, contamination, or evidence of arcing. Watch for abnormal fan operation, burning smell, heat, or unusual sounds during controlled testing. Stop testing immediately if unsafe output behavior is observed.

steps.title: Stop If the Issue Is Resolved
steps.instructions: If the error clears after replacing a faulty accessory, correcting a connection issue, or resolving an external setup problem, perform full functional testing, verify output power using an ESU analyzer, confirm the error does not return, complete electrical safety testing if required, and return the ESU to service only after it passes all required checks.

CCR.Complaint: CONMED System 2450 / 5000 Series ESU reported Err 144. RF Controller and Monitor power levels did not match during activation testing. Unit removed from service for evaluation.
CCR.Cause: Err 144 reproduced during controlled bench testing. External power, accessories, output connections, and basic setup were checked. Fault appears related to internal RF power monitoring, voltage/current feedback, or Controller/Monitor sensing disagreement.
CCR.Resolution: Removed ESU from service and labeled Out of Service. Verified fault with ESU analyzer testing. Recommended bench repair and further evaluation of Controller assembly and RF voltage/current feedback circuits for the affected activation mode.
